desc: Tamarin is a west-coast Mauritius base that balances surf, calm-lagoon swimming, and easy road access to Le Morne and Black River Gorges without the busier north-coast resort pace.
hls: Sunset-facing west-coast beaches with calmer evening sea conditions
Short access to Le Morne, Black River Gorges, and Chamarel day trips
Consistent beginner/intermediate surf options around Tamarin Bay
Strong villa/aparthotel inventory for longer, self-catered stays

todo.name: Tamarin Bay dawn paddle
todo.desc: Join a licensed dawn paddle or kayak slot before trade winds strengthen.

todo.name: Le Morne + lagoon combo day
todo.desc: Pair Le Morne viewpoints with an afternoon swim stop on the southwest lagoon.

todo.name: Black River Gorges trail half-day
todo.desc: Choose a marked trail for endemic forest lookouts and cooler inland temperatures.

todo.name: Chamarel geopark detour
todo.desc: Add Seven Coloured Earth and nearby rum/coffee stops on the same inland loop.

todo.name: Flic-en-Flac evening food run
todo.desc: Go after sunset for street snacks, seafood grills, and a broader dining mix.

todo.name: Responsible dolphin-watch outing
todo.desc: Use operators that cap boat numbers and enforce no-chase wildlife distance rules.

wifi.note: Good in most villas/hotels; verify speeds for remote work stays.

prac.fltNote: Mauritius itineraries almost always route through MRU: nonstop London service is strongest in winter-sun/festive windows, while many UK/Ireland departures in shoulder months connect via Gulf or major European hubs (for example Doha, Dubai, Paris, or Istanbul). Reunion (RUN) only works as a backup when paired with a separate inter-island hop, so missed-connection risk and baggage recheck time are higher. UK demand pressure is usually highest in late July-August and late December.
prac.lang: Mauritian Creole is widely spoken; French and English are common in visitor services.
prac.tz: MUT (UTC+4) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Mauritius allows many tourists visa-free entry for short stays, but return/onward ticket and accommodation proof are commonly required at entry.
alerts: Dolphin excursions in Tamarin Bay should use licensed operators with no-chase policies; avoid boats that crowd pods.
Cyclone-season systems (typically Nov-Apr) can cancel lagoon and boat plans at short notice; keep swap activities ready.
West-coast roads toward Port Louis and Ebene can slow heavily on weekday commute windows; avoid tight same-day flight plans.
Reef/rock sections near some beach entries become slippery at low tide—pack reef shoes for dawn/sunset sessions.

sweet: May-Nov for drier, less humid conditions and reliable west-coast sunsets.
